feature_name: v1.13.0 -- v1.12.0 polish + mobile fixes (subscriptions UI, mobile player, synthetic-folder rename/reorder, filetype dropdown, graceful read-only delete)
base: main (v1.12.0 shipped; prior feature record in docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-06-ytdlp-metube-parity.md)
builds_on: The v1.12.0 yt-dlp MeTube-parity feature (subscriptions + one-shot forms, live status polling, per-sub maxVideos/paused/format/quality, embed metadata, the FR-G display-only synthetic 'Downloads' folder + realpath scan-root hardening). This feature POLISHES that surface + fixes two mobile/UX regressions + one always-on server DELETE UX gap. It does NOT re-litigate v1.12.0's locked decisions (D1-D5) or its security core (T3/T4).
git_ownership_note: Coordinator owns ALL git. SDEs must NOT commit/stage/push. SDE reports files-changed + test output; coordinator commits per task. Node 22 is the test/verify standard.
node_toolchain_note: Node 22 is the toolchain standard. A Node 22 toolchain is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-coder-projects-filetube/139c0e56-b545-4e8e-ba05-f892f6dd6d0d/scratchpad/node-v22.23.1-linux-x64/bin -- build-verify MUST run tests on Node 22, not just fnm's v24.14.0. (fnm PATH export still needed for the v24 default: export PATH="/home/coder/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin:$PATH".)
process_plan: RIGHT-SIZED (not the full v1.12.0 adversarial pipeline). Light Discovery (PM: 5 well-specified items -> concrete ACs; items 1/2/5 are diagnosed fixes, item 3 needs a small PE order/rename design, item 4 needs the args/allowlist + default decision). Then SDE implementation -> build-verify on Node 22 -> a SINGLE quality-assurance review. EXCEPTION: the two flagged items get EXTRA scrutiny (heavier/second-reviewer gate) given the yt-dlp module's C3/C7/E1 history: item 3 (synthetic-folder order/rename WITHOUT reintroducing a db.folders row -- must not regress the disable-no-op or the E1 mount-loss protection) and item 4 (the new filetype/container value flows into yt-dlp spawn args -- same arg-array/`--`/allowlist security bar as v1.12.0 T3/T4, no injection via the filetype value). Dean's on-device pass is the ARBITER for the visual/mobile bits (items 1 + 2). Ship cadence: merge -> tag v1.13.0 -> Docker publish (CI is the image arbiter) -> Dean's on-device pass.

extra_scrutiny_items.item_3_folder_model: Synthetic 'Downloads' folder rename + REORDER in Setup. The synthetic folder MUST STAY OUT of db.folders (that is what prevents the C3/C7 disable-reap/eviction and keeps extraScanRoots the authoritative scan/mount-loss root -- E1). Order for the synthetic entry must therefore be stored SEPARATELY (a folderSettings.order field or a dedicated pref) and merged into the sidebar/Setup display order WITHOUT persisting the folder into db.folders. Do NOT reintroduce a real db.folders row. Reuse the existing real-folder order mechanism; extend it to place the synthetic entry by its stored order. PE designs; heavier review.
extra_scrutiny_items.item_4_spawn_args: New filetype/container dropdown value flows into yt-dlp args (video -> --merge-output-format <ext> and/or --recode-video; audio -> -x --audio-format <fmt>). Validate against an allowlist (reject hostile -> default). Same arg-array via execFile / `--` positional / no-shell discipline as T3/T4. No injection via the filetype value. Store via updateDatabase; backfill existing subs (undefined -> default). Heavier review.

hard_constraints: ADDITIVE / NO REGRESSIONS. The disabled-no-op guarantee for the yt-dlp module UI is UNCHANGED: when FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED is off, the /subscriptions page + new dropdowns + one-shot form + status polling stay absent and FileTube stays byte-identical. Items 1/3/4 touch module-gated code (served from inside the isEnabled gate, NOT public/). Item 5 (DELETE /api/videos/:id) and item 2 (mobile player CSS) are ALWAYS-ON core -- scope tightly, prove non-regression for existing users.
E1 mount-loss protection intact: extraScanRoots stays the authoritative scan/mount-loss root; no scan/prune path may depend on the synthetic folder's presence in db.folders or in GET /api/config.
Security core reuse for item 4: arg-array via execFile (never shell:true), `--` separator, --restrict-filenames kept, cookies redaction on every sink, allowlist-validate the filetype value.
follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM, textContent not innerHTML (KEEP the XSS-safe rendering in subscriptions.js), node:test, lint 0, no new deps.
KEEP the item-1 subscriptions list's XSS-safe textContent rendering + the disabled-absence behavior.

product_forks_for_dean.item_4_default_filetype: RECOMMEND: default VIDEO -> mp4 (best iOS compatibility; webm-on-iOS is the pain being solved) via --merge-output-format mp4; default AUDIO -> mp3. 'default' (yt-dlp's own choice) offered as an explicit dropdown option but NOT the default. PM to confirm; PE to decide merge (--merge-output-format, cheaper/lossless when streams allow) vs recode (--recode-video, guarantees container but costs CPU) -- recommend merge for the mp4 default.
product_forks_for_dean.item_5_remove_anyway: RECOMMEND: YES to a clearly-labeled 'remove from library anyway' option that deletes the db entry even when the file could not be unlinked (e.g. EROFS read-only mount), WITH an honest caveat surfaced to the user that a still-scanned read-only mount will re-add it on the next rescan (UX-clarity fix, not a true removal). The default DELETE still attempts the unlink first and returns a clear, specific message (distinguish EROFS/EACCES 'read-only or permission' from other errors). PM to confirm the copy + whether remove-anyway is a separate request param/endpoint or a follow-up choice in the same flow.

1.title: Fix janky /subscriptions 'Your subscriptions' list (BUG shipped v1.12.0): names/URLs render one-char-per-line
1.root_cause_confirmed: lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js:188 and :361 set row.className='folder-item-row' (a Setup class in public/css/style.css) and the info column uses inline 'flex:1; min-width:0;' (:191/:364). The info column collapses to ~1ch so the name (folder-path-text :194/:367) + URL (word-break:break-all) wrap per character.
1.fix: Give the /subscriptions list its OWN row layout (a dedicated CSS class or corrected inline flex) so the info column takes full available width with normal wrapping (long URLs break-word, not per-char); tidy the add/edit/one-shot option controls (currently cramped). KEEP the XSS-safe textContent rendering + disabled-absence.

2.title: Mobile player oversized (regression)
2.root_cause_confirmed: public/css/style.css:614 .player-container is width:100%; aspect-ratio:16/9 (:616); a max-height:70vh cap at :1681 is likely too tall for a phone portrait.
2.fix: Cap the mobile (portrait) player to a sensible height (target ~40-50vh portrait; reasonable change, Dean tunes on-device). Do NOT break desktop, landscape, or the audio-mode / audio-bg-art layout. Report exact before/after values.

3.title: Synthetic 'Downloads' folder: renamable + reorderable in Setup
3.current_behavior_confirmed: GET /api/config (server.js:1364-1394) derives the download root as a synthetic display-only folder with default name 'Downloads', renamable via a folderSettings entry keyed by the synthetic root (POST /api/config allows this, server.js:1404-1414); it is NEVER written into db.folders. public/setup.html loads data.folders (which INCLUDES the synthetic entry) into configuredFolders (:238) and renders it in the editable list (:256), so it DOES appear + rename persists. BUT reorder does NOT stick: setup reorders by swapping positions in configuredFolders and POSTs that as `folders` (:394); the server strips synthetic roots from db.folders (FIX-2) and re-derives/APPENDS the synthetic entry LAST on the next GET -- so the synthetic folder always jumps back to the end.
3.fix: Store the synthetic folder's ORDER separately (a folderSettings.order field or a dedicated pref) and merge it into the sidebar + Setup display order WITHOUT persisting the folder into db.folders. Reuse the real-folder order mechanism; extend it to place the synthetic entry by its stored order. CRITICAL: keep the synthetic folder OUT of db.folders (E1 / C3 / C7 disable-reap protection); no scan/prune path may depend on synthetic presence.

4.title: Filetype/container dropdown for one-off + subscription
4.current_behavior_confirmed: lib/ytdlp/args.js:361-364 currently forces audio -> ['-x','--audio-format','mp3'] and video -> ['-f', selector] with no container control; --newline added at :360. Subscription record (lib/ytdlp/store.js) has channelUrl/name/format/quality/maxVideos/paused, no filetype/container field.
4.fix: Add a THIRD dropdown (alongside format + quality) for output container/filetype: VIDEO -> mp4 (default) / mkv / webm / 'default'; AUDIO -> mp3 / m4a / opus / 'default'. Wire to args.js: video -> --merge-output-format <ext> (PE decides merge vs --recode-video; default merge->mp4); audio -> -x --audio-format <fmt>. Add a filetype/container field to the subscription record + the one-shot body; validate against an allowlist (reject hostile -> default); backfill existing subs (undefined -> default) via updateDatabase. Add the dropdown to the add + one-shot forms AND the edit (PATCH) path.

5.title: Graceful delete on read-only mounts (server, always-on)
5.current_behavior_confirmed: DELETE /api/videos/:id (server.js) on an unlink failure hits the catch at ~server.js:1831-1836 and returns 500 { error: `Could not delete file: ${err.message}` }, leaving the db completely untouched (the updateDatabase metadata/progress cleanup below never runs).
5.fix: Catch the unlink failure and return a clear, SPECIFIC message (distinguish EROFS/EACCES 'read-only or permission' from other errors). OPTIONALLY (PM decision, recommended YES) support a 'remove from library anyway' that deletes the db entry even though the file could not be unlinked, with the honest caveat that a still-scanned read-only mount re-adds it on the next rescan. KEEP the existing success path + the thumbnail/transcode/db.progress cleanup + the updateDatabase-error handling intact.
files: public/js/main.js or watch.js (delete UX copy, if remove-anyway is user-facing)
5.arbiter: Dean on-device (message clarity) + build/unit tests for the branch logic

history.note: New feature round v1.13.0 'v1.12.0 polish + mobile fixes' initiated by Dean off main (v1.12.0 shipped; prior record in docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-06-ytdlp-metube-parity.md). 5 scope items: (1) fix janky /subscriptions per-char-wrap list, (2) mobile player oversized regression, (3) synthetic 'Downloads' folder rename+reorder in Setup, (4) filetype/container dropdown wired to yt-dlp args, (5) graceful DELETE on read-only mounts. RIGHT-SIZED process: SDE -> build-verify (Node 22) -> single QA review, EXCEPT items 3 (folder-model) + 4 (spawn-args) get extra scrutiny per the yt-dlp C3/C7/E1 history. Dean on-device = arbiter for visual/mobile (items 1+2). Coordinator owns git; SDEs don't commit.

EM VERIFIED every diagnosed root cause against current code: subscriptions.js:188/361 'folder-item-row' + info flex:1;min-width:0 (:191/364) + folder-path-text (:194/367); style.css:614 .player-container / :616 aspect-ratio 16/9 / :1681 max-height:70vh; GET /api/config synthetic 'Downloads' folder (server.js:1364-1394) NOT in db.folders, rename-via-folderSettings allowed (:1404-1414), setup.html renders it (:256) but reorder does NOT persist (server strips synthetic from db.folders + re-appends last on next GET); args.js:361-364 audio -> -x --audio-format mp3, video -> -f selector, no container control; store.js subscription record has no filetype field; DELETE /api/videos/:id catch at server.js:1831-1836 returns 500 + leaves db untouched. Reset state from the completed v1.12.0 feature to this v1.13.0 feature. Wrote the Discovery inbox for product-manager at .state/inbox/product-manager.md (light discovery: 5 items -> concrete ACs; items 1/2/5 as pass/fail fixes, item 3 needs a small PE order/rename design note, item 4 needs the args/allowlist + default decision). Surfaced the two genuine product forks (item-4 default filetype, item-5 remove-anyway) with recommendations. Routed to PM. Awaiting PM output, then /prep-pe-design.

PM wrote docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-v1.13-polish.md covering the original 5 items plus 2 items (6: mobile settings/menu polish for Setup + /subscriptions; 7: drop duplicate Synopsis on downloaded items' watch pages) added by the coordinator after the inbox. Verified item 7's root cause directly (server.js EMBEDDED_TAG_WHITELIST ~line 789 includes 'synopsis'; watch.js renderEmbeddedTags ~line 241 surfaces every non-title/artist tag) and item 6's target files. Produced Goal/Scope/Out-of-scope/Constraints/FR-1..FR-7/NFRs/32 tagged ACs (16 UNIT / 8 INTEGRATION / 8 MANUAL)/Open Questions (both forks resolved: item-4 default mp4/mp3 via --merge-output-format; item-5 remove-anyway=yes as a removeAnyway query param + second-confirm UX flow, recommended copy documented)/Decision log. No new fork surfaced. No conflicts with active exec plans (none), ARCHITECTURE.md, or the tech-debt tracker (item 3 explicitly scoped to not depend on tracker item #10). Set artifacts.requirements and artifacts.exec_plan to the plan path. Design section left for principal-engineer (items 3 and 4 only need real design work; 1/2/5/6/7 are direct-to-implementation). Awaiting EM to route to principal-engineer via /prep-pe-design.
